level_signals: 3+ years focused on fraud, trust & safety, risk, identity, or related domain
Expected to set product vision and multi-year roadmap independently
Base pay range $194,000–$204,500 CAD consistent with Senior PM

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 6+ years of product management experience, including 3+ years focused on fraud, trust & safety, risk, identity, or a related high-stakes platform domain.
including 3+ years focused on fraud, trust & safety, risk, identity, or a related high-stakes platform domain.

Demonstrated ability to set product vision and strategy independently - not just execute on a defined roadmap, but define the north star, build conviction with leadership, and drive a multi-year narrative with measurable goals.

Proven track record driving measurable business outcomes in fraud prevention or risk management - such as reducing fraud losses, improving authorization/approval rates, lowering false positive rates, or optimizing cost per order.

Strong experience working with ML/data science teams on fraud detection models, risk scoring, or decisioning platforms. Comfort with data and experimentation (SQL, analytics tools, A/B testing) to size opportunities, validate hypotheses, and measure impact rigorously.
Comfort with data and experimentation (SQL, analytics tools, A/B testing) to size opportunities, validate hypotheses, and measure impact rigorously.

Demonstrated success working cross-functionally with engineering, operations, data science, finance, legal, and compliance - with strong written and verbal communication skills to produce compelling strategy documents and build confidence in direction.
Bachelor's degree in business, engineering, computer science, or a related field; equivalent practical experience considered.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Experience applying AI/ML innovations to fraud, identity, or trust & safety - such as shipping GenAI/LLM-powered products, building AI-native detection/decisioning systems, or leveraging graph neural networks and multimodal analysis. Strong preference for candidates who think AI-first, not rule-first.

Experience building for multi-sided marketplaces and/or enterprise/white-label platforms, with an understanding of how fraud controls need to adapt for different partner models and user types (customers, shoppers, merchants, advertisers).
Experience leading or influencing without direct authority in an operational environment (e.g., working with Fraud Ops, Trust & Safety Ops, or Risk Operations teams).

explicitly_excluded: Hiring limited to Canada: Ontario, Alberta, British Columbia, and Nova Scotia only. US candidates not eligible despite remote designation.

scope.surface: Fraud detection, identity verification, and appeasements platforms across Instacart's four-sided marketplace (customers, shoppers, retailers, advertisers) and Storefront Pro
scope.business_model: B2C and B2B marketplace (grocery delivery + enterprise/white-label)
core_responsibilities: Own the fraud, identity, and appeasements product vision, strategy, and multi-year roadmap
Drive the evolution of Instacart's real-time fraud detection and identity platform including ML model strategy, decisioning, identity verification, and account integrity
Champion an AI-native, AI-first approach to fraud and trust
Lead the appeasements and order-issue resolution strategy
Be the strategic leader — proactively identify new opportunities and define the direction
Partner with Data Science, ML Engineering, and Fraud Operations to define PRDs and scope ML model improvements
Collaborate across Platform, Payments, Order Management, Shopper, Legal, and CX teams

compensation.equity_form: new hire equity grant and annual refresh grants

green_flags: Equity (new hire + annual refresh) explicitly mentioned
Fully remote role with Flex First policy
Clear product surface with quantified success metrics (fraud losses, cost per order, false positive rates)
Genuine strategic ownership expected — not just executor role
High-impact domain: directly drives cost savings and revenue growth across large-scale marketplace
red_flags: Hiring restricted to four Canadian provinces (Ontario, Alberta, British Columbia, Nova Scotia) despite #LI-Remote tag — eliminates most remote candidates
Compensation stated in CAD only; USD equivalent not provided, making cross-market comparison harder
Broad scope (fraud + identity + appeasements + AI strategy) may indicate under-resourced team or high bandwidth demands
No mention of team size or reporting structure
